title: Private billing rates via access code and driver user group
goal: Enable private billing rates that are only available to eligible drivers, with eligibility determined by access code assignment and/or driver user-group membership, so pricing can be segmented without exposing those rates publicly.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal admin can create a private billing rate/profile that is not exposed to general drivers by default
Portal admin can grant access to a private rate by driver user-group membership
Portal admin can grant access to a private rate by access code
A clear deterministic precedence model exists when a driver qualifies for multiple private/public billing profiles
Eligible drivers receive the correct private rate during reservation/session pricing resolution
Ineligible drivers cannot see or receive the private rate
Access code usage is validated and auditable
Effective billing profile/rate is snapshotted at session start so historical sessions do not change when rules are edited later
Existing public/default billing behavior remains unchanged for drivers without private access

inScope: Define private billing rate/profile eligibility model
Use driver user-group membership as one eligibility source
Use access code as another eligibility source
Add portal admin management for private rates and eligibility assignment
Integrate private-rate resolution into session/reservation pricing selection
Add auditability for access-code use and resolved rate selection
Ensure private rates are hidden from non-eligible drivers in relevant user-facing surfaces
outOfScope: A full coupon/promo engine with arbitrary campaign logic
One-time discount vouchers beyond access-code eligibility for a private rate
Complex stacking of multiple private pricing modifiers in v1
General access control / permissions beyond pricing eligibility

decisions.decision: Treat private rate access as an eligibility layer on top of billing-profile resolution
decisions.rationale: This keeps the feature aligned with existing/default pricing instead of introducing a separate disconnected pricing engine.

decisions.decision: Require deterministic precedence between access-code-derived access, user-group-derived access, and default/public rates
decisions.rationale: Without explicit precedence, drivers qualifying through multiple paths will get surprising or unstable pricing outcomes.

decisions.decision: Snapshot effective private/public billing selection at session start
decisions.rationale: Billing history must remain stable even if access codes, group memberships, or private-rate configs change later.

assumptions: Billing profiles or an equivalent pricing abstraction either already exist or can be extended to support private/public designation
Driver user groups will exist or be introduced as part of TASK-0201-related work
Access code entry/association can be integrated into the relevant driver/admin flow without breaking current activation UX
Reservation pricing and session-start pricing share enough of the billing-resolution path to make private-rate resolution consistent
implementationPlan: 1. Confirm the pricing abstraction to extend: billing profile, tariff profile, or equivalent
2. Define eligibility model: private rate linked to user groups, access codes, or both
3. Define exact precedence for default/public rate vs group-based private rate vs access-code-based private rate
4. Add schema for private-rate metadata and access-code entities/usages if needed
5. Add portal admin CRUD for private rates and their eligibility mappings
6. Integrate driver-facing/private-rate visibility rules so non-eligible drivers cannot see private pricing
7. Integrate pricing resolution into reservation/start-session path and snapshot result at session start
8. Add audit logs or read models for why a private rate was applied
9. Run QC: typecheck, eligibility proof, ineligible-proof, snapshot proof, regression proof for public pricing
filesExpectedToChange: packages/api/src/routes/services related to pricing, sessions, reservations, and admin config
packages/mobile and/or driver-facing API responses if private-rate visibility affects surfaced pricing
risks: If precedence is unclear, drivers may receive the wrong rate when they match multiple eligibility paths
If private rates leak into general availability/read models, non-eligible drivers may see restricted pricing
If access code usage is not audited, support and billing disputes will be hard to resolve
If private-rate selection is not snapshotted, historical session pricing can drift after config changes
This feature depends conceptually on user-group billing work; implementing it first may create rework if TASK-0201 changes the billing model
openQuestions: Should access codes attach to a driver account persistently, one session at a time, or a fixed expiry window?
If both group and access code qualify a driver for different private rates, which wins?
Should private rates be site-specific, organization-wide, or both?
At what point does a driver enter an access code: profile, reservation modal, session start, or admin assignment?
Should private rates affect reservation fees, activation fees, energy rates, idle fees, or all billing components?

proofOfWork.summary: Created task contract for private-rate pricing with explicit dependency on deterministic billing-profile resolution, access-code auditability, and snapshot integrity.
proofOfWork.artifact: Task spec defines eligibility sources (access code and user group), precedence requirement, visibility constraints, and session snapshot requirement.
